source.description: Six-panel marker process contact sheet generated for an honest Doodlea.day archive backfill, then cropped into public step frames and a finished image.
source.notes: Chosen as a simple non-face marker sticker subject that differs from recent beach, firework, food, gadget, and speech-bubble lessons.

process_strategy: A six-panel process builds a potted cactus sticker from pot and cactus lobes, then adds arms, spines, flower, marker fills, shine gaps, and final outline cleanup.

final_elements.name: small flower pot
final_elements.description: A squat trapezoid pot with a thick rim.

final_elements.name: main cactus stem
final_elements.description: A tall rounded cactus stem rising from the pot.

final_elements.name: side cactus arms
final_elements.description: Two rounded side arms attached to the main cactus.

final_elements.name: spine marks
final_elements.description: Short black dash spines placed on the existing cactus shapes.

final_elements.name: top flower
final_elements.description: A small flower on the cactus top.

final_elements.name: green and terracotta marker fills
final_elements.description: Bright green marker on the cactus, terracotta on the pot, pink on the flower, and gray sticker shadow.

final_elements.name: white shine gaps
final_elements.description: Small shine gaps on the existing marker-filled cactus and pot.

frames.visible_job: Places the pot rim, tapered pot body, and base guide.
Adds the tall rounded cactus stem rising from the pot.
Adds two rounded side arms attached to the main cactus.
Adds small spine dashes to existing cactus parts and a little top flower.
Adds green, terracotta, pink, gray, and shine accents only to shapes already present.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, deepens the marker fills, sharpens shine gaps, and strengthens the sticker shadow.

rejection_checks: No new pot, cactus arm, spine system, flower, or color area should first appear in the final image.
The cactus must keep the same pot, stem, arm placement, scale, and front-facing angle in every frame.
The finish must look like a handmade marker sticker, not a clean vector icon or polished poster.
